name: Andouille in Comforting Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and cook until they are clear or tender.
3. Add celery, bell pepper, and parsley; stir occasionally.
4. Stir in garlic and cook for a few minutes longer.
5. Add steak sauce, hot sauce, ketchup, cayenne pepper, and salt to taste.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2-3 hours until thickened.
7. Cool slightly before combining with 1 cup of the prepared sauce.
8. Slice 1 lb Andouille sausage into thin rounds (about 1/4 inch thick).
9. Combine slices with the sauce and heat thoroughly on the stovetop or in a chafing dish.
10. Serve hot over French bread or use teethpicks to spear the sausage for easy eating.
11. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator; they will keep for several weeks.
"Other smoked sausages may be used, but we like Andouille."
